entry.title: Re: Can we create our own dpi in the sustainability contr...
entry.content:
Hi Narendra, I hope you're doing great! 

I am Kauê from Sustainability SCT/SFM support team! 

In SAP Sustainability Control Tower, you cannot create a completely new DPI object with its own ID. The product provides a fixed set of DPIs, and all data imports must follow one of these structures.

Instead, extend existing DPIs:

  • Create custom dimensions via “Manage Custom Dimensions” app and assign to DPIs.​

  • Create custom measures via “Manage Measures” linked to those DPIs/dimensions.

  • Build custom metrics (your KPIs) on top via “Manage Metrics”.​

This allows customer‑specific data models without new DPI types.

Official SAP Help Page SCT docs:

  • #Data Provider Interfaces (DPIs).

  • #Manage Custom Dimensions.

  • #ESG Data Model Basics.

entry.title: Re: Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
@sivakami_plr You will need 3 formations.

@timmybecker you can delete the cockpit and re-create it, however, keep in mind that the actual tenant is not deleted for approx 30 days so if you plan to use the same subdomain (i believe within the same region), you won't be able to do so.

entry.title: Re: How to create new condition tables for batch determin...
entry.content: Hi, the fields are pre-delivered by SAP. It is not possible for the customers to add further fields to the list of selectable key fields. You can get more details on the KBA "3267138 - Condition records and Batch determination in Public Cloud", for a list of available key fields. If your use case requires expanded configuration, we recommend submitting a request via the Customer Influence Portal . Please follow KBA "2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product" for detailed steps.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Create Custom Analytical Query in SAP S/4HA...
entry.content: Good day, thank you for your response and the references provided. I have verified the first point you mentioned. We are using the new application (Custom Analytical Queries and Services) and not the deprecated one. However, the “Create” button is still disabled in our Production tenant. Regarding authorizations: We reviewed the catalog SAP_BW_BC_AQD_PC, and it is already assigned in Production with the same configuration as in Development. In our Development (Customizing) tenant, we tested assigning the standard role BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST, and we are able to create Custom Analytical Queries without any additional changes. This confirms that the functionality works correctly in DEV using the standard SAP role. However, in Production, even with equivalent permissions assigned, the “Create” option remains disabled. Could this behavior be related to: A restriction at tenant level? , IAM App activation differences between tenants?, Or a governance configuration preventing creation directly in Production? We would appreciate your guidance on what additional configuration we should verify. Best regards, Octavio

entry.title: Re: Discount on invoices
entry.content:
Hello @Vir4-Ji_ ,

There is no direct KZBTR-equivalent indicator field in ACDOCA.
Discount identification in S/4HANA must be done via:

-Cash discount amount fields (SKNTO etc.)
-Payment terms (ZTERM)
-Discount G/L account postings
-Clearing document references

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Technical Fields & SAP Notes
In BSEG I can see field KZBTR (cash discount indicator), but not in ACDOCACorrect. In S/4HANA, BSEG is no longer the leading source for reporting. The Universal Journal (ACDOCA) replaces many classical fields, and some BSEG indicators are not directly replicated as standalone flags.
Is there a direct “discount indicator” field in ACDOCA?❌ No single equivalent field like KZBTR exists in ACDOCA. Discounts are reflected through amounts and condition logic, not through a simple indicator field.
How are cash discounts represented in ACDOCA?Cash discounts are reflected through: • Fields: SKNTO (Cash Discount Amount in Document Currency) • Separate line items posted to discount accounts (e.g., revenue reduction or expense reduction). • Clearing information (REBZG, AUGBL, AUGDT).
How to identify if an FI invoice includes cash discount terms?Check: • Payment terms (ZTERM) in ACDOCA/BKPF context • Baseline date (ZFBDT) • Discount amounts SKNTO / SKNT2 / SKNT3 fields (depending on level).
How are logistics invoice (MM) discounts represented?Logistics discounts (pricing conditions) are usually reflected as: • Reduced invoice amount at posting time • Separate condition-based line items if configured They are not stored as a simple indicator in ACDOCA.
Can I identify discount by G/L account?✅ Yes (recommended method). Identify discount accounts (e.g., cash discount revenue/expense accounts). If document contains posting to those G/L accounts, discount was applied.
How to detect statistically in reporting?Use combination of: • ACDOCA-SKNTO (cash discount amount) • ACDOCA-HKONT (discount G/L account) • Clearing fields (AUGBL) to detect discount realized at payment.
Why no direct KZBTR equivalent?S/4HANA redesign removed redundant indicators. Universal Journal stores actual values and accounting impact rather than flags. Reporting should rely on amounts and account postings.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Universal Journal Entry (ACDOCA) SAP Help → Cash Discount Posting Logic in Accounts Payable / Receivable
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Version Applicability)📌 SAP Note 2156822 – Universal Journal Field Mapping (BSEG to ACDOCA) 📌 SAP Note 2349297 – Cash Discount Handling in S/4HANA Finance 📌 SAP Note 2431747 – Reporting Differences Between BSEG and ACDOCA 📌 SAP Note 1976487 – Cash Discount Posting Logic in FI

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Manual Rework activity appearing in production Manage...
entry.content:
Hello @R_Pooja ,

Manual Rework tasks are system-specific and triggered by upgrade consistency checks.
Differences between Q and P data can cause the task to appear only in Production.
Even if master data is already maintained, you must review and confirm the rework activity to close it.

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Required Action & SAP Notes
Manual Rework activity appears after 2602 upgradeDuring major releases (e.g., 2602), SAP delivers configuration or master data consistency checks. If Production data does not fully align with new logic, a Manual Rework task is triggered automatically.
Why only in Production and not in Quality?In 2SL landscape, Q and P systems may have slight master data or configuration differences at upgrade time. The rework check runs system-specifically. If Production had documents, transactional data, or historical setup not present in Q, the check may trigger only in Production.
Master data already maintained, but still showing Action RequiredThe rework check validates specific technical attributes (e.g., new fields, flags, consistency rules). Even if business data exists, the new required attribute might be missing or not migrated automatically.
Is action required if data seems correct?✅ Yes, you must open the rework task and validate each item. Even if data appears maintained, SAP requires confirmation to mark the rework as completed. Otherwise, the task remains open.
Could it be a false positive?Occasionally, yes. Some upgrade checks are triggered based on system conditions. If data is already compliant, you can confirm and complete the rework activity manually.
Why triggered after 2602 specifically?Release 2602 likely introduced new validation logic or mandatory fields in configuration/master data. Production-specific data inconsistencies can trigger rework tasks post-upgrade.
What happens if no action is taken?System remains operational, but the rework task stays open and may cause audit/compliance warnings during future upgrades. Best practice is to review and complete it.
Recommended Action1️⃣ Open the rework activity in Manage Your Solution 2️⃣ Review impacted objects 3️⃣ Validate master data attributes 4️⃣ Confirm completion in app
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Upgrade and Release Management in S/4HANA Cloud SAP Help → Manual Rework Activities After Upgrade
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Applicability for 2602 Release)📌 SAP Note 3116400 – Manual Rework Activities After Upgrade in S/4HANA Cloud 📌 SAP Note 2831001 – Upgrade-Related Configuration Consistency Checks 📌 SAP Note 3090106 – Release Upgrade Impact in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Budget exceeded error BP603, WBS element - PS Module
entry.content:
Hello @cciol ,

Check where budget is assigned (project vs WBS level).
Ensure budget is distributed to the sender WBS.
Review control profile (OKOB) for hierarchical check settings.
Confirm value categories in allocation structure match the budget category.

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Root Cause & SAP Notes
WBS element uses another WBS (same project) as allocation senderIn PS, budget is controlled at WBS level according to Availability Control (AVC) settings. Even within the same project, each WBS has its own budget and assigned value categories.
BP603 error occurs even though budget seems availableBP603 is triggered because the sender WBS does not have sufficient available budget in the relevant value category at the time of allocation. The system checks the sender WBS individually, not the overall project total.
Exceeded amount equals allocation valueThis indicates the sender WBS has zero available budget in the controlled value category. The system cannot “pull” budget from another WBS automatically within the same project.
Why does it work when using WBS from another project and disabling AVC (CJBW)?Disabling Availability Control removes the budget check, so allocation posts successfully. This confirms the issue is AVC logic, not allocation configuration.
Why same-project WBS behaves differentlyWithin the same project, budget distribution and control profile may restrict budget usage to the specific WBS element. If budget is assigned at higher level (e.g., project definition or parent WBS) and not distributed downward, child WBS cannot consume it.
What to check✔ Budget distribution (CJBU / budget supplement) ✔ Control profile in OKOB ✔ Assigned value categories ✔ Budget tolerance limits ✔ Whether budget is assigned at parent but not released to child
Key Technical LogicAvailability Control checks table BPJA (budget) vs actuals/commitments in COSP/COSS. It evaluates WBS element individually unless control profile allows hierarchical consumption.
Recommended FixOption 1: Distribute budget to sender WBS. Option 2: Adjust control profile to allow hierarchical availability check. Option 3: Review value category assignment in allocation structure (OKO6).
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help Portal → Project System → Budget Management → Availability Control SAP Help → Budget Distribution in WBS Elements
Relevant SAP Notes (Check Version Applicability)📌 SAP Note 360216 – BP603 Budget Exceeded Message Explanation 📌 SAP Note 548013 – Availability Control Logic in Project System 📌 SAP Note 175784 – Budget Distribution and Hierarchical Control 📌 SAP Note 1606807 – Budget Availability Issues in Internal Allocations


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Running SAP AMS Like a Product, Not a Support Factory
entry.content:
Very well articulated. The point about “optimizing for closure, not stability” really stands out.

 

I have seen similar patterns in AMS where SLA dashboards look green, but repeat incidents during critical cycles like month-end erode business confidence.

 

Measuring recurrence at process level instead of only response time is a powerful shift. Strong reminder that stability is an outcome, not an activity.
